deleteStorageConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this storage? This action cannot be undone.
transferStorageDescription: Transfer this storage to another workspace
migrateStorageDescription: Migrate backups from this storage to another storage
testConnectionViaButton: - test connection via button below (even if you updated settings);
waitUntilNextSave: - wait until the next save is done without errors;
removeStorageConfirmation: Are you sure you want to remove this storage? This action cannot be undone. If some backups are using this storage, they will be removed too.
storageDescription: Storage - is a place where backups will be stored (local disk, S3, etc.)
storageUsedByDatabases: This storage is used by some databases. Please transfer or remove related databases first.
selectWorkspaceToTransfer: Select a workspace to transfer this storage to.
azureStorageConnectionStringFromAzurePor: Azure Storage connection string from Azure Portal
customEndpointUrlOptionalLeaveEmptyForSt: Custom endpoint URL (optional, leave empty for standard Azure)
optionalPrefixForAllBlobNames: Optional prefix for all blob names (e.g., 'backups/' or 'my_team/')
howToUseWithCloudflareR2: How to use with Cloudflare R2?
skipTLSCertificateVerification: Skip TLS certificate verification. Enable this if your S3-compatible storage uses a self-signed certificate. Warning: this reduces security.
optionalPrefixForAllObjectKeys: Optional prefix for all object keys (e.g., 'backups/' or 'my_team/'). May not work with some S3-compatible storages. Cannot be changed after creation (otherwise backups will be lost).
useVirtualHostedStyleUrls: Use virtual-hosted-style URLs (bucket.s3.region.amazonaws.com) instead of path-style (s3.region.amazonaws.com/bucket). May be required if you see COS errors.
customS3CompatibleEndpointUrlOptionalLea: Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL (optional, leave empty for AWS S3)
pasteYourRcloneConfContentHere: Paste your rclone.conf content here. You can get it by running 'rclone config file' and copying the contents. This config supports 70+ cloud storage providers.
contentIsHiddenToNotExposeSensitiveData: *content is hidden to not expose sensitive data. If you want to update existing config, put a new one here
optionalPathPrefixOnRemoteWhereBackupsWi: Optional path prefix on remote where backups will be stored (e.g., '/backups' or 'my-folder/backups')
enableSslTlsEncryptionForSecureConnectio: Enable SSL/TLS encryption for secure connection
windowsDomainNameOptional: Windows domain name (optional, leave empty if not using domain authentication)
remoteDirectoryPathForStoringBackups: Remote directory path for storing backups (optional)
useExplicitTlsEncryptionForSecureFileTra: Use explicit TLS encryption (FTPS) for secure file transfer
pasteYourSshPrivateKey: Paste your SSH private key (PEM format). Supports RSA, DSA, ECDSA, and Ed25519 keys.
skipSshHostKeyVerification: Skip SSH host key verification. Enable this if you trust the server. Warning: this reduces security.
systemStorageTooltip: System storage is accessible by all workspaces in this instance. Regular storage is only accessible by current workspace.
googleDriveStorageConfigNotFound: Google Drive storage configuration not found
googleDriveStorageConfigNotFoundInDto: Google Drive storage configuration not found in DTO
oauthParamNotFound: OAuth param not found. Ensure the redirect URL is configured correctly.
